title: Check Reachability

content.problemStatement:
You are given a graph of NNN vertices - numbered from 111 to NNN and MMM edges between them. 

The ithi^{th}ith edge connects the AiA_iAi​ node to BiB_iBi​. The edges are directed.

You live at the vertex 111, output all the vertices that you can visit. You can travel using the given edges.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a two integers NNN and MMM, denoting the number of vertices and edges.
Next MMM lines describes the edges. i−thi-thi−th of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ denoting there exists a directed edge from AiA_iAi​ to BiB_iBi​.


Output Format
Output all the nodes you can visit from the node 1, in increasing order of their index.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
The sum of M over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and M — the number of vertices and the number of directed edges. Then M lines follow, each containing two integers A and B denoting a directed edge from A to B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output all vertices reachable from vertex 1 (including 1 itself) in increasing order, separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case.

solution.approach: The graph is directed. Starting from vertex 1 we perform a depth‑first (or breadth‑first) search, marking every visited vertex. Because we only need the set of reachable vertices, the order of traversal does not matter. After the search finishes we simply iterate from 1 to N and output the vertices whose visited flag is true. The adjacency list representation gives O(N+M) time per test case and O(N+M) memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N+M) per test case, O(total N+total M) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N+M) for the adjacency list and the visited array
